linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: Not a mechanical MOAT port: heavydb JIT-compiles each SQL query via in-process LLVM NVVM->NVPTX (NativeCodegen.cpp triple nvptx64-nvidia-cuda, nvvm.annotations, libdevice.10.bc, lookupTarget(nvptx64), CGFT PTX emit) then CUDA-driver JIT-links PTX+cuda_mapd_rt.fatbin (NvidiaKernel.cpp cuLink*) and launches via cuLaunchKernel. ROCm needs a new LLVM-AMDGPU codegen path (amdgcn, ocml/ockl, code-object emit) + a hipModule runtime + a ~70-file CUDA-driver-runtime port + a warpcore CG-hashtable port -- upstream compiler-backend scale. It also will not compile against the host ROCm LLVM 22 (codegen uses LLVM-14 APIs removed in 22: PassManagerBuilder.h, CGFT_*), and that LLVM 22 is the only one here with the AMDGPU target (no NVPTX). No static GPU kernel and no isolated GPU unit test, so no correctness-first slice for the validator. The analogous Intel SPIR-V/Level-Zero backend was attempted and abandoned (closed unmerged PRs; inert L0 stub only). See plan.md; needs jeff's fund-vs-skip decision.
